title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani)
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for a satisfying lunch or dinner. Get the full step-by-step guide!
seo.h1: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
h2Headings: How to Make Butter Chicken: Step-by-Step
Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cream and cilantro, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich and creamy flavors of homemade Indian Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani – a true culinary delight!

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a beloved Indian dish featuring succulent chicken pieces simmered in a rich, creamy tomato-based sauce. Its mild spice profile and velvety texture make it a popular choice globally. This recipe brings you the authentic taste of India right to your kitchen, perfect for a comforting meal.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 min

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1/2 tsp turmeric powder, 1 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder, 1 tsp garam masala, salt to taste, and lemon juice. Mix well, cover, and marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp oil and 1 tbsp butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and cook in batches until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per batch. Remove cooked chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining 2 tbsp butter. Once melted, add the chopped onion and sauté until golden brown and softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add 1 tbsp fresh ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer and Blend Gravy Ingredients
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews, 1.5 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder, 1/4 tsp turmeric powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1/2 tsp cumin powder, and 1/2 cup water. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10-12 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.

instructions.title: Achieve a Smooth Gravy
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the cooled gravy mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth and creamy. For an extra silky texture, you may pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve, pressing down on any solids.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish the Dish
instructions.text: Return the smooth gravy to the pan. Add the cooked chicken, sugar, and crushed Kasuri Methi (fenugreek leaves). Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the gravy's flavors. Stir in the fresh cream just before serving. Do not boil after adding cream.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve your authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani hot with basmati rice, naan bread, or roti. Enjoy this rich and flavorful Indian classic!

tips: For the most tender chicken, marinate it overnight. The yogurt helps to tenderize the meat beautifully.
Do not skip soaking the cashews; it helps achieve a truly smooth and rich gravy texture, preventing any graininess.
Always add fresh cream at the very end and avoid boiling the gravy after its addition, as this can cause the cream to curdle.
article:
Butter Chicken, known in India as Murgh Makhani, is more than just a dish; it's a culinary hug, a symphony of rich flavors that has captivated palates worldwide. This iconic Indian lunch recipe offers a perfect balance of creamy texture, tender chicken, and aromatic spices, making it a beloved choice for both seasoned curry enthusiasts and those new to Indian cuisine. Its vibrant orange hue and irresistible aroma promise a comforting and deeply satisfying meal, perfect for a midday treat or a hearty dinner.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, literally translates to "chicken with butter." Originating in the 1950s at the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi, this dish was serendipitously created when chefs combined leftover tandoori chicken pieces with a rich tomato-based gravy made with butter and cream. The result was an instant classic.

At its heart, Butter Chicken features succulent pieces of chicken (often marinated in yogurt and spices) swimming in a luscious, velvety gravy. This gravy is typically made from a base of pureed tomatoes, onions, and cashews, enriched with copious amounts of butter and cream. A blend of mild spices like garam masala, fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), and a touch of sugar creates its signature sweet and tangy profile. Unlike many other Indian curries, Butter Chicken is not aggressively spicy, making it incredibly approachable and a favorite for many, including children. Its mildness allows the complex flavors of the spices, the sweetness of the tomatoes, and the richness of the dairy to truly shine through.

## Ingredients for Butter Chicken

Crafting the perfect Butter Chicken involves a thoughtful selection of ingredients. Here's what you'll need:

**For the Chicken Marinade:**
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken (thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces)
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   Salt to taste

**For the Gravy:**
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter (or more!)
*   1 tablespoon oil
*   1 large onion, roughly chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 (28-ounce) can crushed tomatoes or 4-5 large fresh tomatoes, pureed
*   1/2 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15-20 minutes)
*   1/2 cup water
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder
*   1 teaspoon coriander powder
*   1/2 teaspoon cumin powder
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 teaspoon kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   1-2 teaspoons sugar (to balance acidity)
*   Salt to taste
*   1/2 cup heavy cream (plus more for garnish)

**Garnish:**
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped
*   A swirl of heavy cream

## How to Make Butter Chicken: Step-by-Step

Creating this restaurant-quality Indian lunch recipe at home is simpler than you might think. Follow these steps for an authentic Murgh Makhani:

**Step 1: Marinate the Chicken**
In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, and salt. Mix well to ensure every piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally, 2-4 hours (or even overnight for maximum flavor).

**Step 2: Cook the Chicken**
Heat a tablespoon of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 5-7 minutes per side, until browned and cooked through. The chicken doesn't need to be fully cooked at this stage, as it will finish cooking in the gravy. Remove the chicken and set aside.

**Step 3: Prepare the Gravy Base**
In the same pan, add 1 tablespoon of butter. Once melted, add the chopped onions and sauté until translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the pureed or crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews, and 1/2 cup water. Bring to a simmer. Add red chili powder, coriander powder, and cumin powder. Cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have softened and the mixture thickens slightly.

**Step 4: Blend the Gravy**
Remove the pan from heat and let the mixture cool slightly. Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender (or use an immersion blender) and blend until absolutely smooth. For an extra silky gravy, pass the blended mixture through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan, pressing down on the solids to extract all the liquid. Discard any remaining pulp.

**Step 5: Finish the Gravy**
Return the pan with the strained gravy to medium heat. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter, garam masala, crushed kasoori methi, sugar, and salt to taste. Stir well and simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the gravy to thicken to your desired consistency.

**Step 6: Combine and Serve**
Add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering gravy. Stir gently to coat the chicken. Pour in the heavy cream and stir until combined. Simmer for another 2-3 minutes, just enough to heat the chicken through and allow it to absorb the gravy's flavors. Do not boil vigorously after adding cream, as it can curdle.

Garnish with fresh cilantro and a swirl of extra cream before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken

Achieving that perfect, restaurant-quality Butter Chicken at home is all about a few key techniques:

*   **Marination Time:** Don't rush the marinade. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight is ideal.
*   **Quality Chicken:** Use boneless, skinless chicken thighs for the most tender and juicy results. Chicken breast can work but tends to dry out more easily.
*   **Smooth Gravy is Key:** Blending the gravy base until perfectly smooth, and then straining it, is crucial for that signature velvety texture. Don't skip this step if you want a truly luxurious sauce.
*   **Kasoori Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves):** This spice is non-negotiable for authentic Butter Chicken flavor. Lightly crush it between your palms before adding it to release its aromatic oils.
*   **Balance of Flavors:** The touch of sugar helps balance the acidity of the tomatoes, while the butter and cream provide richness. Don't be afraid to taste and adjust the seasonings (salt, sugar, spices) to your preference.
*   **Charred Chicken (Optional but Recommended):** If you have a grill or can use a broiler, giving the marinated chicken a slight char before adding it to the gravy imparts a wonderful smoky depth of flavor, reminiscent of tandoor-cooked chicken.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs beautifully with a variety of accompaniments, making it a complete and satisfying Indian lunch recipe.

*   **Breads:** The classic pairing is with warm, fluffy naan bread, especially garlic naan, which is perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich gravy. Roti, chapati, or paratha are also excellent choices.
*   **Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or a fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a light contrast to the creamy curry and helps soak up the sauce.
*   **Sides:** A simple side salad, a cooling raita (yogurt dip with cucumber and mint), or some pickled onions can cut through the richness and add a refreshing element to your meal.
*   **Beverages:** A sweet mango lassi or a simple glass of water complements the flavors beautifully.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the traditional Butter Chicken recipe is perfect as is, there are several ways to adapt it to dietary needs or personal preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Murgh Makhani:** Easily transform this into Paneer Makhani by substituting the chicken with cubes of paneer (Indian cheese), or Tofu Makhani using firm tofu. Simply pan-fry the paneer or tofu until golden before adding to the gravy.
*   **Healthier Options:** Reduce the amount of butter and cream, or use low-fat alternatives. You can also bake or air-fry the chicken instead of pan-frying to reduce oil.
*   **Spice Level:** For those who prefer more heat, increase the amount of red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili to the gravy base.
*   **Nut-Free:** If you have a cashew allergy, you can substitute cashews with blanched almonds or sunflower seeds, or simply omit them. The gravy will be slightly less thick and creamy, but still delicious. You could also use a tablespoon of cornstarch slurry at the end to thicken.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** For an extra layer of smoky flavor without a tandoor, place a small piece of hot charcoal in a small heatproof bowl inside the finished curry. Drizzle a teaspoon of ghee or oil over the charcoal, cover immediately, and let it infuse for 5-10 minutes before removing.

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

**Is Butter Chicken spicy?**
No, traditional Butter Chicken is generally mild. The red chili powder used (especially Kashmiri chili powder) is often for color rather than heat. Its flavor profile is sweet, tangy, and creamy, making it very palatable for those who prefer less spice. You can, of course, adjust the heat level to your liking.

**Can I make Butter Chicken ahead of time?**
Absolutely! Butter Chicken is one of those dishes that often tastes even better the next day as the flavors have more time to meld and deepen. Prepare the entire dish, let it cool, and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop.

**Can I freeze Butter Chicken?**
Yes, Butter Chicken freezes very well. Once completely cooled, transfer it to freezer-safe containers and freeze for up to 2-3 months. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator and reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a splash of water or cream if needed to restore consistency.

**What makes Butter Chicken different from Chicken Tikka Masala?**
While both are creamy, tomato-based chicken curries, there are key differences. Chicken Tikka Masala typically features chicken tikka (pieces of chicken that have been marinated and grilled, often with a smoky char) in a gravy that can be slightly spicier and often includes bell peppers and onions. Butter Chicken, on the other hand, traditionally uses un-charred chicken and has a sweeter, richer, and creamier gravy, with cashews and fenugreek leaves being more prominent.

**Why is my Butter Chicken not bright orange?**
The vibrant orange color often comes from using Kashmiri red chili powder, which imparts a beautiful color without excessive heat. The intensity of your tomatoes can also play a role. If you're using a different type of chili powder, or very pale tomatoes, the color might be less vibrant. Some restaurants may also use a tiny amount of food coloring, but this is not necessary for a delicious homemade version.
